title: lupinus sellulus subsp. ursinus
intro: Lupinus sellulus subsp. ursinus, also known as the Sierra lupine, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the lupin family and is native to the Sierra Nevada mountain range in California, USA. The Sierra lupine has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of lupine include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support bone health, making it a valuable herb for individuals with conditions such as arthritis and osteoporosis. Lupine can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
